subject: Enforcement directive regarding emerging waste burning in Ward W219 (Samaypur Badli), Zone Z06
background: An emerging waste burning source covering 9 cells was detected in Ward W219 (Samaypur Badli), Zone Z06, over a period of 30 fire hours. Monitoring data recorded an AQI of 374 in the Very Poor category, a PM2.5 concentration of 215.5, and an atmospheric boundary layer height of 120m. The detection reflects a fire fraction of 0.1786 with an Absorbing Aerosol Index at the 70th city percentile.
finding: The gathered evidence supports the presence of open waste burning with a confidence score of 0.76. This finding is established by the highest deterministic score of 3.45 for waste burning alongside trapped air conditions caused by low boundary layer height.
directive: The competent local authority is directed under Rule 15(zg) of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016 to immediately prohibit the open burning of solid waste in streets and open spaces within Ward W219. Action must be taken under Section 31A of the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981 to issue directions for the regulation or prohibition of contributing processes. Additionally, strict enforcement of restrictions under the Graded Response Action Plan (CAQM) corresponding to the Very Poor AQI band must be implemented.
evidence_chain: Highest deterministic score of 3.45 for waste burning
Trapped air with low boundary layer height of 120m

subject: Enforcement directive regarding indicative open waste burning in Ward W110 (Madanpur Khadar), Zone Z04
background: An emerging waste burning event spanning 16 cells was detected in Ward W110 (Madanpur Khadar), Zone Z04, active over a duration of 2 hours. The surrounding area recorded a PM2.5 level of 181.1 and an AQI of 348 in the Very Poor category, accompanied by a boundary layer height of 120 m and elevated NO2 levels.
finding: The gathered evidence, including a deterministic score of 1.238 and elevated NO2 concentrations at the 96th city percentile, supports the occurrence of waste burning. Given the calculated confidence level of 0.48, this finding is indicative and requires on-site verification.
directive: The local authority is directed to enforce Rule 15(zg) of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016 to prohibit open burning of solid waste on streets and open spaces. Necessary measures and directions must be executed under Section 31A of the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981, alongside implementing relevant Stage escalation restrictions under the Graded Response Action Plan (CAQM) corresponding to the Very Poor AQI band.

subject: Enforcement action under Graded Response Action Plan (CAQM) for acute Very Poor air quality in Zone Z01
background: An acute air pollution event was recorded across 13 cells in Zone Z01, located in an unassigned ward outside city limits. The recorded PM2.5 level reached 189.6 µg/m³, yielding an AQI of 354 categorized as Very Poor, with a boundary layer height of 150 meters under trapped air conditions. Zero fire hours and a fire fraction of 0.0 were recorded for the area.
finding: The evidence supports traffic as the potential source, based on a highest deterministic score of 0.303 and wind alignment reaching up to 0.97 across candidate sources. Because the confidence level is 0.14, this finding is indicative and requires verification on site.
directive: In accordance with the Graded Response Action Plan (CAQM) provision on stage escalation by AQI band, designated officers must enforce graded restrictions on construction, DG sets, and dust-generating activity matching the Very Poor AQI band. Field personnel are directed to undertake physical verification in Zone Z01 to confirm the indicative traffic source and monitor local compliance.

background: Monitoring in Ward W223 KAPASHERA, Zone Z05, recorded a PM2.5 concentration of 230.5 and an AQI of 385, falling under the Very Poor category across 15 cells. The chronic measurement indicates a boundary layer height of 130 meters, with zero fire hours and a fire fraction of 0.0.
finding: The evidence indicates traffic as the likely source, based on a category score of 0.798, an NO2 column value of 117.6, and 7 landuse traffic sources within 1 km. Because the confidence level is 0.5, this finding is indicative and requires verification on site.
directive: Pursuant to Stage escalation by AQI band under the Graded Response Action Plan (CAQM), the addressee is instructed to perform immediate on-site inspection in Ward W223 KAPASHERA. Upon site verification, the addressee must implement the applicable graded restrictions on construction, DG sets, and dust-generating activity.
